title: Ties That Bind
description: A series of new works on paper that embody just some of the many women (past and present) I have crossed paths with. Moments captured in passing, encounters that are burned into memory, flashes of personality exploding through outfits, nails, accessories and more. These fellow “Croydonites” have shaped, inspired, traumatised & enlightened me…

title: Baby Café with Three Feathers Company
description: It looks like nothing was found at this location. Maybe try one of the links below or a search?

title: Spiritual Tiik’s Tibetan Sound Bowl Meditation
description:
This meditation class will create a deep relaxation allowing you to feel calm and have a clear mind.

Benefits of Sound Healing: – Reduce stress and anxiety – Improve your sleep – Feel relaxed and calm – Quiets the mind and allow for awareness of thoughts – Release stagnate energy in body & much more…

Who is Spiritual Tiik:

NATURAL EMPATH, REIKI MASTER TEACHER, TRANSFORMATIONAL SPEAKER & COACH, THETA PRACTITIONER, AUTHOR, ENTREPRENEUR, AND RISING GALACTIC STARSEED INCARNATED TO SUPPORT INDIVIDUALS TO IGNITE THEIR TRUE POWER AND REDISCOVER THEIR PURPOSE.

Please bring a yoga mat or something comfortable to lay on.

Doors Open: 6:40pm, please be on time for a prompt 7pm start

Cost: £14 (advance online booking)

OTD (if space available) – £15

PLEASE NOTE: If you are sensitive to loud sounds this event may not be for you. While sound baths are generally safe, they may not be suitable for everyone. If you have a pacemaker, heart monitor, seizure disorder, are pregnant, have hearing sensitivities, or experience anxiety or PTSD, we recommend checking with your healthcare provider before attending. Some people may experience dizziness, emotional release, or discomfort from certain frequencies. Feel free to contact us with any concerns before the event.

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help!

title: Life Drawing (Untutored)
description:
Stanley Life Drawing is a fortnightly untutored life drawing session with male or female nude models at Stanley Arts. All levels of experience welcome, materials provided.

Further info:

Enquiries – stanleylifedrawing@gmail.com

Social media: @stanleylifedrawing

Advanced online booking: £11

On The Door Price (If space available): £13

Book multiple sessions at once to save on the Booking Fee (£1.25 per transaction).

For concession tickets please show proof of ID (Student card or Universal Credit letter) upon arrival.

Who are Stanley Life Drawing?

Clare Haward – Artist/Art Tutor – @clare_haward – www.clarehaward.co.uk

Hannah Lee Miller – Animator/Illustrator – @thegreatescapist – www.hannahleemiller.co.uk

Rebecca K Jones – Illustrator/Comics Artist – @rebecca_k_jones – www.rebeccakjones illustration.com

Peter Morey – Cartoonist/Graphic Scribe – @petermoreysketches – www.petermorey.com

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help!

title: CRoydon Emerging Writers (CREW)
description:
CREW (CRoydon Emerging Writers) is a peer-led writers group that is open to all.

CREW was founded in order to bring together both aspiring and experienced writers alike, to allow them to collaborate, gain feedback on their work, and talk in a safe and non-judgemental space with other like-minded creatives.

This writing group supports all forms of written creativity – novelists, playwrights, poets and more are all welcome. CREW is a space to meet other writers, learn, share and collaborate. And it’s free!

Find out more about CREW by visiting our Community post here.

Please contact crew@stanleyarts.org for more information.

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help!

title: Hartbeeps
description:
### Hartbeeps Magical Classes for Babies and Toddlers

- Location Society Room

- Date Jul 9

- Time 10:00 am till 1:30 pm

- Tickets £14 drop in, £7 sibling. £11/week when you book the term, rolling so you can join at any time.

- Ages 8 weeks - 5 years old

- Book your place

### The original musical story tellers and creators of multi-sensory play for babies and young children, you have arrived in the right place and are in great hands.

Step inside, take a wander around and have a magical time!

Timetable:

10am Happy House (for toddlers and their younger siblings)

11:15 Baby Beeps (for babies that are sitting confidently or crawling)

12:30 Baby Bells (from newborn to sitting)

10am

Join Dexter Cat & Adrian Mouse in the Hartbeeps Happy House from 10am. Participants take a different sound journey every week, as we set the scene and your Little Hartbeepers build their story! It’s fun fun fun all the way, with our own original versions of classic nursery rhymes, the most fabulous songs, gorgeous puppet characters, dress-up, props and more…

11:15am

All aboard the Baby Beeps Babble Express, next stop Babble Station! Sessions offer BIG fun in full colour, with larger than life puppet characters, rhymes, raps, claps and rhythmic beats; supporting baby’s blossoming speech and language development.

12:30pm

Baby Bells classes are designed to ignite and delight those freshly acquired senses and help baby to uncurl and release trapped energy. Sessions focus on bonding and building confidence.

- Baby Beeps, for babies from 7 months approx. (for babies that are sitting unaided)

- Baby Bells, for babies from 8 weeks approx. (younger babies very welcome)

- Hartbeeps are currently running a promotion for babies under 8 weeks to join us for free in our Baby Bells class at 12:30, using the promo code UNDER8WEEKS to claim.

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help!

Classes are held in spaces throughout Stanley Arts. Details in confirmation email.

title: Cherrystars Performing Arts
description:
Cherrystars are an award winning, community performing arts school. Their team consist of BRIT School alumni and professional television and theatre actors who specialise in teaching innovative performing arts classes to the early years.

Star in their magical movies and stage shows building confidence, creativity and community!

The core values at Cherrystars are Communication, Creativity, Confidence Building and Community. They have taken great pride over the last decade, to have provided a safe, nurturing and affirming place in the local community for children to unleash their creativity and develop a love of the arts.

Visit the Cherrystars website today.

Follow Cherrystars on Instagram, Facebook & X (FKA Twitter) for the latest news.

title: Jazz On A Summer’s Day
description:
South Norwood Jazz Club – Jazz On A Summer’s Day

An afternoon of Swingin’ classic Jazz at the outdoors Stanley Arts Yard Bar, with a bar serving hot drinks, cold drinks and snacks.

This is an all ages event, both adult and child tickets are available. Babes in arms do not need to be booked a ticket, please only book a child ticket if they require a seat.

* * *

Doors 1.30pm

Music from 2pm

* * *

Kitty Whitelaw – Vocals

Alex Bryson – Piano

Asaph Tal – Double Bass

Joe Dessauer – Drums

Magnus Pickering – Trumpet

* * *

More musicians to be announced!

title: Our Community Planting Day
description:
This summer, Stanley Arts is bringing the Courtyard Garden back to life through our Green Spaces, Green Minds project.

Following our consultation workshop, Goldfinch Garden Design are now developing the final design using the ideas and inspiration shared by the community.

We’re now looking for local volunteers to help bring the garden to life and keep it thriving.

You can sign up to a morning or afternoon timeslot (or join for both!) via our new SignUp Genius landing page for volunteers, and take part in the planting day doing activity that you feel comfortable and confident to do.

Refreshments will be provided throughout the day and pizzas will be ordered for lunch!

Stay for the Garden Launch Celebration

Once the planting is complete, we’d love you to join us from 3.00–4.00pm as we celebrate the launch of the new Courtyard Garden. Enjoy a drink from the bar, see the new garden and spend some time enjoying the transformed space with neighbours, volunteers and the wider Stanley Arts community – everyone is welcome.

We’d love to build a team of local volunteers to help care for the garden this summer. If you’re interested in becoming a Garden Volunteer to help us water and care for the new garden, please sign up here.
